fit_meta.note: Measured-geometry rail WOT fit (alpha 0.4, rail/tap fric 0.1). BEST-ACHIEVABLE points: fitted row [87.5, 91.8, 116.6, 112.6, 110.7, 109.8] vs stock [103.7, 115.7, 111.3, 110.1, 108.7, 106.0] -> r 0.05, max_norm_shape_err 0.176, peak 5300 vs stock 3900: the r>=0.95 gate is NOT met. 2700/3900 are base-lever CEILINGS (b130; lower bases REVERSE), 6900 is a floor (b177 blows up), i.e. the low-rpm charge deficit of the measured geometry is outside the EXVANOS lever range. Ratio-wise 4600-6900 sit at 1.02-1.05 of stock (legacy datum was 0.79-0.87 everywhere). Part-load calibration is insulated by the p=VE/VE_WOT normalization. Header-length hypothesis falsified (650mm primaries: 2700 -5.9pp, 6900 blow-up).

note: ve_by_rpm = the OFFICIAL app-path wot_quick row at 60-cycle decks (phase3_wot_quick_measured.json) -- the same denominators the app's wot_ratio_maxdp uses. 3100 runs at the interpolated base 130.
